reasoning: The informal statement claims: if f is an isometry on a compact metric space X, then f is bijective and hence a homeomorphism. The Lean statement sets up X as a compact metric space and f : X → X an isometry (matching the informal hypotheses exactly), and concludes `Continuous f ∧ Bijective f ∧ IsOpenMap f`. For a self-map between topological spaces, the conjunction "continuous + bijective + open map" is logically equivalent to "is a homeomorphism" (since a continuous bijection has continuous inverse iff it is an open map). Thus the Lean conclusion exactly captures "f is a bijection and a homeomorphism" — it is a standard packaging of the homeomorphism condition rather than a strengthening or weakening. Hence the Lean statement is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
